title: Daily Briefing — Tuesday, June 2
tldr: Play: make GitHub Copilot follow your conventions automatically with a custom-instructions file. One small file in your repo and every suggestion respects your stack, style, and rules — no more re-explaining in each prompt.
The day's headlines (an executive order, a hyperscaler's in-house models, cloud-marketplace availability) were adoption news — cut. The durable dev play is below.

sections.h: Run it today: teach Copilot your project's rules once

blocks.sub: Stop fixing the same Copilot mistakes in every file
blocks.p: Payoff: suggestions that already use your framework, naming, test style, and 'never do X' rules — fewer rewrites and more consistency across the team.
blocks.doIt: Create .github/copilot-instructions.md at your repo root. Write plain-English rules: language/framework versions, naming conventions, your testing library, formatting, and hard always/never rules (e.g. always use async/await, never callbacks; validate all inputs with zod). Commit it. Copilot Chat now attaches it to every request in that repo — expand the References on a reply to confirm it loaded. Docs: GitHub — repository custom instructions.
blocks.note: Keep it short and concrete; vague rules get ignored. Path-specific rules go in .github/instructions/.

sources: GitHub Docs — Repository custom instructions
